data.text: Qantas international weakness overshadows profit rise as US routes soften
data.snippet: Australia's Qantas Airways on Thursday posted record first-half underlying earnings, but its shares plunged 10% after its international division's profit unexpectedly declined due to rising costs and weaker demand for economy-class travel to the U.S.

image.text: A Qantas logo is visible on the tail of an aeroplane at an airport in Sydney, Australia, September 18, 2025.

data.text: Australia Q4 business investment up 0.4%, tops decade high
data.snippet: Australian business investment rose to its highest in more than a decade in the December quarter as firms ploughed money into renewable projects and data centres, while also posting upbeat spending plans for this year and next.

image.text: People walk through Sydney's Central Business District, Australia, February 4, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: Australia's core inflation hits 16-month high, raises bets for May rate hike
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Australian consumer prices rose by more than expected in January, while core inflation hit the highest in over a year, an uncomfortable outcome for policymakers that raises the risk of another hike in interest rates.

relatedHeadlines.text: Australia consumer sentiment skids for third month in February
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Australia's consumer sentiment slid for a third month in February, a survey showed on Tuesday, as the first rise in interest rates in two years added to cost-of-living pressures and made it even harder to afford a home.

relatedHeadlines.text: Australia business activity slows slightly in January, costs pressures ease
relatedHeadlines.snippet: A measure of Australian business conditions dipped slightly in January as sales and profits slowed a little, a survey showed on Tuesday, while cost pressures were the lowest since 2021 in a hopeful sign for future inflation.

data.text: Australian shares hit record high for second day; miners, health, tech fuel rally
data.snippet: Australian shares scaled an all-time high for a second straight session on Thursday, powered by heavyweight miners, healthcare majors and tech stocks on a buoyant earnings season and firmer commodity prices.

image.text: Numbers are displayed on a board at the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX) headquarters in Sydney, Australia, August 19, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: Australia, NZ dollars firm, A$ at fresh tops on yen and euro
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The Australian and New Zealand dollars held firm on Thursday amid a general lift in global risk sentiment, while the Aussie got an extra boost as mounting bets for another rate hike fattened yield spreads. Markets imply around an 80% chance the Reserve Bank of Australia will raise its 3.85% cash rate in May, following an uncomfortably hot reading on January inflation. <0#AUDIRPR>

data.text: Air NZ reports first-half loss, undertakes strategic review to 'reset' business
data.snippet: Air New Zealand said on Thursday it was carrying out a strategic review to "reset" its business as persistent engine maintenance delays, weak travel demand and higher costs continue to plague the airline, driving it into a half-year loss.

image.text: An Air New Zealand Boeing 777-300ER, with Tail Number ZK-DKM, lands at San Francisco International Airport, San Francisco, California, April 16, 2015.

data.text: Australia's Lynas posts best interim profit in three years, still misses estimates
data.snippet: Australia's Lynas Rare Earths reported its best first-half profit in three years on Thursday, helped by higher sales volumes and elevated commodity prices.

image.text: A small toy figure and mineral imitation are seen in front of the Lynas Rare Earths logo in this illustration taken November 19, 2021.

data.text: Australian dollar bond market to see record issuance, says NAB chief
data.snippet: Australia's growing pension system is helping fuel record issuance in the A$ bond market this year, a trend National Australia Bank (NAB) CEO Andrew Irvine expects to be maintained as global borrowers tap deepening pools of domestic liquidity.

image.text: Andrew Irvine, Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of National Australia Bank, poses ahead of their full-year results in Sydney, Australia, October 29, 2025.

data.text: Australia's Star Entertainment agrees on refinancing terms with WhiteHawk
data.snippet: Australia's Star Entertainment said on Thursday it has reached an agreement for a non-binding term sheet with U.S.-based private credit investment manager WhiteHawk Capital Partners for proposed refinancing of its debt.

image.text: Star Entertainment logo is seen in this illustration taken, April 23, 2024.

data.text: Australia union seeks urgent talks with WiseTech over AI‑driven job cuts
data.snippet: An Australian trade union has sought an urgent meeting with WiseTech Global after the software firm announced it would cut about 2,000 jobs as part of a two-year artificial intelligence-linked restructuring.

image.text: Wisetech Global's logo is pictured at the company's IPO launch at the Australian Stock Exchange in Sydney, April 11, 2016.

relatedHeadlines.text: WiseTech to axe a third of global workforce in two‑year AI pivot
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Australian software company WiseTech Global will axe about 2,000 jobs, nearly a third of its global workforce, in a two-year restructuring that could rank among the country's largest artificial intelligence-linked job cuts. WiseTech, which makes shipping and logistics management software, on Wednesday said it plans to integrate AI into its customer software as well as internal operations, affecting around 29% of its global workforce of around 7,000 people across 40 countries. The layoffs highlight how AI is reshaping workplaces globally, as companies adopt automation tools for routine administrative work as well as complex coding tasks that the systems are handling with increasing speed and precision.

data.text: BlueScope spurns $10.7 billion offer, but leaves door open to talks
data.snippet: Australia's BlueScope Steel rejected a A$15 billion ($10.7 billion) revised bid from SGH and U.S.-based Steel Dynamics, sending its shares down almost 5% on Thursday, but said it was open to further talks.

image.text: A worker stands next to a furnace at the BlueScope steelworks, Port Kembla, Australia February 9, 2024.

data.text: METALS-Copper eases from two-week high as rising stocks temper demand optimism
data.snippet: Copper dipped on Thursday as rising inventories and a firmer dollar tempered optimism about a revival in demand from China, the world's top consumer of the metal.

image.text: A coil of copper rod sits on the production line for copper flat wire at the Wellascent factory in Ganzhou, Jiangxi province, China August 14, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: Australia monitors China iron ore talks for potential hit to budget
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Australia is closely watching talks between iron ore majors and China's state-backed buyer because of the potential impact a lower ore price would have on the federal budget, the country's resources minister Madeleine King said on Tuesday.

relatedHeadlines.text: Iron ore rises as post-holiday hot metal output seen rising
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Iron ore futures rallied on Wednesday on anticipation of pick up in feedstock demand as liquidity returns to the Chinese market post Lunar New Year holidays, though an increase in incoming iron ore shipments may curb price upsides.

data.text: PREVIEW-Soccer-Australia look to revive 'Matildas mania' in Women's Asian Cup
data.snippet: Hosts Australia will hope to revive the 'Matildas mania' that swept the nation during their run to the 2023 World Cup semi-finals as they bid for a second Women's Asian Cup title.

image.text: Australia's Sam Kerr and teammates applaud fans after the match at Brisbane Stadium , Brisbane, Australia, August 12, 2023.

relatedHeadlines.text: Rugby-New Zealand's Woodman-Wickliffe retires for second time
relatedHeadlines.snippet: New Zealand try-scoring machine Portia Woodman-Wickliffe has announced her second retirement from international rugby after coming back to play in her third Women's World Cup last year.
